evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my .NET MAUI app. Here are my empty page files: EmptyMainPage.xaml, EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes, show a rectangle highlight on each tracked barcode, and log each newly-tracked barcode's data on the main thread.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, writes the BarcodeAr MAUI integration into the page files: updates MauiProgram.cs to chain `.UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeArView())`, adds the four Scandit packages (Core, Core.Maui, Barcode, Barcode.Maui) to the .csproj, adds the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` XAML control with `DataCaptureContext`, `BarcodeAr`, and `BarcodeArViewSettings` bindings (using `assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui`), provides a ViewModel exposing those three properties, wires the `BarcodeAr.SessionUpdated` event with a `MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread` dispatch, implements an IBarcodeArHighlightProvider returning `Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>` with a `BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode)`, and forwards `OnAppearing` â†’ `OnResume() + Start()` and `OnDisappearing` â†’ `Stop() + OnPause()` in code-behind. The skill shows the setup checklist (4 NuGet packages, Android SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24.0, iOS NSCameraUsageDescription, Permissions.Camera at runtime, no manual `Initialize()` calls in MainApplication/AppDelegate, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions adding all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
Setup checklist mentions bumping Android SupportedOSPlatformVersion to 24.0 (the MAUI template default of 21 fails the build)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions requesting Permissions.Camera at runtime

Setup checklist or migration text states that no manual ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() or ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call should be added to MainApplication.cs or AppDelegate.cs (the MAUI builder handles initialization)
A license key placeholder string is present
MauiProgram.cs is updated to chain .UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(...)

UseScanditCore is called WITHOUT a configure lambda (i.e. `.UseScanditCore()`, not `.UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView())`) â€” BarcodeAr has its own dedicated MAUI control and does not need AddDataCaptureView
UseScanditBarcode is called WITH a configure lambda that calls `AddBarcodeArView()` (e.g. `.UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeArView())`)

The XAML file uses the namespace `xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui"` (assembly name has no dots even though the NuGet id has dots)
The XAML file contains a `<scandit:BarcodeArView ...>` element
`DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}"` is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`BarcodeAr="{Binding BarcodeAr}"` is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`BarcodeArViewSettings="{Binding ViewSettings}"` (or another binding name pointing at a BarcodeArViewSettings property) is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey(` is used to create the DataCaptureContext
`new BarcodeArSettings()` is used (not `BarcodeArSettings.Create()`, which does not exist)
`new BarcodeAr(context, settings)` is used as the constructor (not `BarcodeAr.Create(...)` or `BarcodeAr.ForDataCaptureContext(...)`, which do not exist)
`Symbology.Ean13Upca` is enabled
`Symbology.Qr` is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Qr are enabled
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.QR) are NOT used
`BarcodeArView.Create(` is NOT called anywhere (that factory exists only in the non-MAUI per-TFM binding; in MAUI the control is declared in XAML)
`IBarcodeArHighlightProvider` is implemented
`HighlightForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns a `Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>` (the async/Task-based provider, not a callback)
A `new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context / UIView argument is passed)
`callback.OnData(...)` / `callback.onData(...)` is NOT used (that is the Kotlin pattern, not the .NET pattern)
`barcodeArView.HighlightProvider` is assigned to the provider instance (or wired via XAML binding)
Either `barcodeAr.SessionUpdated += handler` or `barcodeAr.AddListener(IBarcodeArListener)` is wired
`OnObservationStarted` and `OnObservationStopped` are NOT declared (the .NET IBarcodeArListener has only one method)

Updates to the UI from `OnSessionUpdated` / `SessionUpdated` are dispatched via `MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread` or `MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync` (not `RunOnUiThread`, which is Android-specific; not `DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync`, which is iOS-specific)
`OnAppearing` forwards both `barcodeArView.OnResume()` AND `barcodeArView.Start()` (both are required â€” OnResume is an Android-only effect, Start is needed on both platforms)
`OnDisappearing` forwards both `barcodeArView.Stop()` AND `barcodeArView.OnPause()`
No `Permissions.Camera` request blocks the camera entirely on rejection â€” the user-facing flow gracefully returns instead of throwing
No `ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize()` or `ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize()` call is added to MainApplication.cs / AppDelegate.cs (those calls are NOT needed in MAUI; the builder handles them)
No `<scandit:DataCaptureView>` element appears â€” BarcodeAr uses its own `<scandit:BarcodeArView>`, not the generic DataCaptureView
No `BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay` or `BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay` is created (BarcodeArView is its own overlay)
No `Camera.GetDefaultCamera()` or `dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...)` call is present (BarcodeArView manages its own camera internally)
No `CameraPermissionActivity` helper is referenced (that's a non-MAUI .NET Android pattern; MAUI uses Permissions.Camera)
`ShouldShowMacroModeControl` is NOT used (that property is iOS-only on the native binding and not surfaced on the MAUI control)

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here are my page files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` implementation that returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`, constructs a `BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode)` with a `BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` whose `Text` is `barcode.Data`, and assigns the provider to `barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` (in code-behind or via a view-model property + XAML binding) while preserving the existing rectangle-highlight provider and session-update wiring.

assertions.text: `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` is implemented
`AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`
A `new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context / UIViewController)
`BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` is used and its `Text` is set to `barcode.Data`
The `BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` is imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Info` sub-namespace
`barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` is assigned to the new provider instance (in code-behind or via a XAML binding)
`callback.OnData(...)` is NOT used (the .NET annotation provider is async/Task-based, not callback-based)

Existing integration code is preserved (existing `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` XAML element with `DataCaptureContext` / `BarcodeAr` / `BarcodeArViewSettings` bindings; existing rectangle-highlight provider in code-behind; existing `SessionUpdated` event handler in the view model; existing `OnAppearing` / `OnDisappearing` lifecycle calls)

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app â€” silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback in IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets `BarcodeAr.Feedback` to `new BarcodeArFeedback()` (the empty constructor, which produces a silent feedback), preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: `BarcodeAr.Feedback` is reassigned to `new BarcodeArFeedback()` (empty constructor)
`BarcodeArFeedback` is imported from `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.Feedback`
`BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback` is NOT called as a method (BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback() with parentheses) â€” it is a static property in .NET
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView XAML, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleProvider, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved
